category_title: Students with a history of school failure and deep mistrust of the adult world
minimal_context: The student is finishing a morning coffee break in the school kitchen and is asked to transition into the classroom for formal instruction.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Break time is over, so please finish your coffee and head into the classroom for the morning lesson.

STUDENT: I'm not going in there. It was tough at home last night and I haven't slept well.

TUTOR: We need to start the lesson now, so please come inside.

STUDENT: Why should I? The only thing I know I can do in there is fail. I'm terribly good at that.

TUTOR: You just need to sit at your desk and try.

STUDENT: No. You're not listening to me. It doesn't matter what I say because everyone is against me, just like at every student welfare conference I've ever been to.

options.A: Takes the coffee cup from the student's hand and places it next to a desk inside the classroom.
options.B: Stretches the rules a little, such as giving the student permission to sit in the breakout room.
options.C: Immediately removes all demands and expectations to enter the classroom for a period of 60 seconds.
options.D: Develops a transition plan: identifies the morning lesson theme, labels the classroom materials, and asks the student how to engage.
